qEnglish: The farmer fastened the bull tightly to the stake. Choose the correct question for this sentence:
explanationEnglish: The original statement is in the simple past tense ('fastened'). Its question counterpart must use 'did + subject + base form of verb (do)'.
subject: English

qEnglish: She sold all the articles for no profit. Choose the right question for this sentence:
optionsEnglish: Did she sell all the articles for no profit?
Did she sold all the articles for no profit?
Does she sell all the articles for no profit?
Do she sells all the articles for no profit?
explanationEnglish: The sentence is in the simple past tense ('sold'). The interrogative form requires 'Did + subject + base verb (sell)'.
subject: English

explanationEnglish: Both 'although I was very tired' (conjunctional clause) and 'in spite of being very tired' (prepositional phrase) are grammatically correct options.
subject: English

qEnglish: He doesn't behave well _______ he hopes to get votes.
explanationEnglish: 'Nevertheless' serves as a concessive adverbial link meaning 'in spite of that', fitting the contrastive logical flow of the sentence.
subject: English

qEnglish: Before the bell rang, the class was _______ to go home.
explanationEnglish: 'All ready' is a phrase meaning completely prepared. 'Already' is an adverb meaning before this time, which does not fit contextually.
subject: English

qEnglish: Choose the correct option for the underlined part: We _have had_ his speech yesterday.
explanationEnglish: The indicator 'yesterday' makes present perfect 'have had' incorrect; it must be replaced with the simple past form 'had'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Choose the correct option for the underlined part: Let _he and I_ do the work today.
explanationEnglish: The word 'let' is followed by object pronouns, so 'he and I' must change to 'him and me'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Identify the grammatically most appropriate sentence from the options:
optionsEnglish: In a week's time I will have completed my work.
In a week's time I will be completed my work.
In a week's time I will be complete my work.
In a week's time I will complete my work.
explanationEnglish: 'In a week's time' establishes a future deadline, making the future perfect construction 'will have completed' the most suitable choice.
subject: English

qEnglish: Identify the most appropriate sentence from the following options:
optionsEnglish: He had been living in London for two years when I visited him.
He had lived in London for two years when I visited him.
He lived in London for two years when I visited him.
He was living in London for two years when I visited him.
explanationEnglish: An action that was continuously taking place over a period of time up to another event in the past is best expressed in the past perfect continuous tense.
subject: English

qEnglish: Which of the following is not a simple sentence?
optionsEnglish: He is happy because he passed the exam.
explanationEnglish: 'He is happy because he passed the exam' contains a principal clause and a subordinate clause linked by 'because', making it a complex sentence rather than a simple one.
subject: English

qEnglish: The teacher has instructed all of us to finish the work by tomorrow, _______?
explanationEnglish: A positive sentence using the present perfect auxiliary 'has' takes a corresponding negative question tag formed with the same auxiliary: 'hasn't he?'.
subject: English

qEnglish: Choose the simple sentence from the options below:
optionsEnglish: You may do it when you please.
You may do it at your pleasure.
It pleases you and it your pleasure.
You may do it because it pleases you.
explanationEnglish: 'You may do it at your pleasure' contains only one finite verb phrase and one independent clause, fulfilling the criteria for a simple sentence.
subject: English

qEnglish: Which of the following is a correct sentence?
optionsEnglish: Do you like to play Badminton?
explanationEnglish: 'Do you like to play Badminton?' correctly follows standard subject-verb agreement rules for questions, using the base infinitive accurately.
subject: English

qEnglish: Which of the following Tag Questions is correct for the sentence: 'This soup is very hot, _______?'
explanationEnglish: A positive statement with the verb 'is' requires a matching negative question tag using the corresponding pronoun: 'isn't it?'.
subject: English
